content.full_text:
Re
F arn P olrrrcAI- P RACTTcEs C oulrrssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 . Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
July 7,2005
Eric Miller, President
Eric Miller Architects, Inc.
157 Grand Avenue, Suite 105
Pacific Grove, CA 93950
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. W-05-141
Dear Mr. Miller:
This letter is in response to your request for advice (dated July 5, 2005) regarding
the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 This letter is
intended to further clarify that the Commission cannot issue advice within the time
constraints that you desire, nor will it render written advice regarding past conduct.
(Regulation 18329.) You stated that ameeting regarding the matter for which you
requested advice is scheduled for tonight (July 7,2005).
To the extent that you may have received informal assistance, rather than formal
written advice pursuant to section 83114, it does not provide immunity from future
prosecution for violation of the Act should it later be determined that you participated in
a decision in violation of the Act's conflict of interest provisions. Consequently, it would
be prudent to refrain from participating in any such decision without obtaining formal
written advice. If the decision has been put over to a future meeting, please contact us.
Otherwise, your request will be considered withdrawn.
Please note, this letter is a public record and will be retrained in the Commission's
files with your original request for advice.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-s660.
sgrdx,
"'-"' 
\ c!t' )''*
Luis6 Menchaca
General Counsel
i { (-(( (.
1 Go,re-ment Cocle sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether the requester can obtain formal FPPC advice regarding conflict-of-interest issues under the Political Reform Act within an expedited timeframe for a same-day decision.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC cannot provide formal written advice within the requested timeframe or regarding past conduct. Informal assistance does not provide immunity from prosecution, and the requester should refrain from participating in the decision without obtaining formal written advice or request that the decision be postponed.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a declination/limitation letter rather than substantive advice. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. The letter explains FPPC procedural constraints (Regulation 18329) and advises the requester to either postpone the decision or obtain formal written advice first.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the requester can obtain formal FPPC advice regarding conflict-of-interest issues under the Political Reform Act within an expedited timeframe for a same-day decision.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC cannot provide formal written advice within the requested timeframe or regarding past conduct. Informal assistance does not provide immunity from prosecution, and the requester should refrain from participating in the decision without obtaining formal written advice or request that the decision be postponed.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines to issue timely formal advice on a conflict-of-interest question due to the same-day meeting deadline and clarifies that informal assistance does not provide prosecutorial immunity under the Political Reform Act.
